ARE.definition: ARE is CLAIRE Systems' chronological external memory authority: an append-style JSONL memory primitive that records timestamped, hash-fingerprinted text outside the model so CLAIRE can recall ordered prior experience without making the language model own or rewrite memory.

origin.source: Original ARE implementation and CLAIRE architecture authority docs

evidence.summary: The preserved original AREStore.ingest(text) creates records with ts, sha, and text, writes them to JSONL, and last_n(n) recalls records chronologically.
append_original_are_memory(text) preserves the original ARE record shape and read_original_are_history(limit) reads ordered JSONL records with malformed rows quarantined.
ARE is registered as the default runtime memory authority with memory_authority=true; Trace, Veritas, and CourtListener are explicitly not memory authorities.
The documented rule says ARE is the chronological memory authority for CLAIRE and must not be replaced by vector search, SQLite, model memory, or subsystem logs.

ARE.safety_rule: ARE records may be recalled as governed memory leads, but raw memory must not be dumped into normal user-facing answers. Lane, scope, authority, Diode, Sentinel, and Trace still govern use.
